filter_index.pickup_city: Shalamar Road, Indira Chowk, Ranbir Market, Jammu City
filter_index.video_title: Brammah Valley Trek | Jammu and Kashmir's New Trek | Indiahikes

snow_or_rain_notes: Snow in upper reaches in May and June (not necessarily on the trail)
No snow on the trail in September
Odd snowfall on high mountain peaks as autumn arrives
Light showers expected at end of June
Occasional showers possible in early September

terrain_challenges: Long trekking days of 8 to 10 km, sometimes 12 to 13 km
Multiple stream crossings with no bridges requiring nimble footwork
Narrow ledge sections (not too technical, guided by team)
Last two days are long and all downhill, hard on knees
altitude_or_weather_risks: Summit reaches 11,600 ft; start around 6,000 ft; gain about 2,000 ft per day, which helps acclimatization
Altitude sickness is less likely due to slow gain
Above 10,000 ft, risk of Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS) is real and can affect any trekker regardless of fitness
Rainfall or snowfall lasting hours can change trek dynamics; rain can flood trails or make them slushy, snowfall can obstruct paths requiring fresh tracks
Kedar Maidan campsite sits on a river delta and can be flooded by heavy rain

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog or run 5 km in under 35 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level)
Build cardiovascular endurance and muscle strength
Train core, calves, glutes, quadriceps, and hamstrings
approval_requirements: Screenshots must demonstrate meeting the trek's fitness criteria
Runs must meet the trek's fitness criteria at least 3 times in the past month
prep_window: 6 weeks (Indiahikes free 6-Week Training Plan designed by Prathima Chhabria and Gautam Singh)
Upload fitness screenshots 15 to 20 days before the trek date
Runs must meet criteria at least 3 times in the past month

experience_themes: Greener valley with patches of snow in upper reaches in May and June
Clearer skies with light showers expected at end of June
Trekking under shade of tall pine trees next to the gushing Nath River
Lush green valley after monsoons in September
Clearer air offering better views of surrounding landscapes in September
Crisp chill in the air as autumn arrives
primary_watchouts: Very short accessibility window during the year
Light showers at end of June
Long trekking days of 8 to 10 km, sometimes 12 to 13 km
Multiple stream crossings with no bridges requiring nimble footwork

video_title: Brammah Valley Trek | Jammu and Kashmir's New Trek | Indiahikes
